text.title: Mocking Strategies Worked Example
text.summary: Worked examples in Python (autospec + boundary mock), TypeScript (Vitest module mock + clearAllMocks), and Go (interface substitution).

example.note: Python — patch where used (r3), autospec to catch typos (r2), boundary mock (r1).
example.code: def test_payment_retries_on_timeout(mocker): mock_post = mocker.patch( "myapp.adapters.stripe.requests.post", # patch where it's looked up autospec=True, # signature drift catches typos ) mock_post.side_effect = [Timeout(), Timeout(), Mock(status_code=200)] result = PaymentService().charge(amount=100) assert result.success is True assert mock_post.call_count == 3

example.note: TypeScript Vitest — module-level mock with clearAllMocks per test (r5).
Go — interface substitution (the language-native mocking style).
Bad — bare MagicMock no spec, patches origin not call site.
example.code: mock = MagicMock() # no spec → typos silently absorbed (r2 violation) mocker.patch("stripe.Charge.create") # patches origin → live API still hit (r3 violation)
